pick.rationale: Michigan enters as the clear favorite in this Midwest Regional Final, leveraging their #2 national ranking with an elite 31-2 record, top-10 offense, and the nation's 2nd-ranked defense. The efficiency gap is substantial, with Michigan's +38.38 adjusted efficiency margin significantly exceeding Iowa State's roughly 5th-7th ranked profile featuring a 27-7 record and elite defense but weaker offense. While Iowa State's defensive prowess under T.J. Otzelberger presents legitimate upset danger in this 1 vs 2 seed matchup, Michigan's superior offensive firepower and overall quality should prove decisive in this Elite 8 contest.

reasoningStep.title: Michigan vs Iowa State Elite 8 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Michigan holds a substantial advantage as the #2 nationally ranked team with elite two-way play against Iowa State's roughly #5-7 profile featuring elite defense but weaker offense.
reasoningStep.evidence: Michigan ranks #2 in KenPom/Torvik with 31-2 record, 8th-ranked offense (126.7 adj_o), and 2nd-ranked defense (88.4 adj_d) with +38.38 efficiency margin. Iowa State finished 27-7 (3rd in Big 12), ranked 7th in AP Poll, with 21st-ranked offense (120.0 ORtg) and 6th-ranked defense (95.6 DRtg). Michigan advanced through the bracket with high confidence picks (96% R64, 84% R32, 73% Sweet 16) while Iowa State had more competitive wins (89% R64, 72% R32, 56% Sweet 16 vs Virginia). The roughly 10-15 point efficiency margin gap and Michigan's superior offensive rating should prove decisive against Iowa State's elite but not quite Michigan-level defense.

Gaming Insider Sign In Tom Wilkinson March 17th 2026 - 6 Min Read Midwest Region 2026: Odds, Predictions & Schedule Basketball • NCAAB The Midwest Region of the 2026 NCAA Tournament features a compelling mix of national title contenders, dangerous mid-seeds and potential Cinderella stories that could shake up the road to the NCAA Men's Final Four . Michigan enters the bracket as the No. 1 seed and one of the most complete teams in college basketball, powered by a dominant frontcourt and a balanced roster capable of controlling games inside and out. But this region is far from a one-team show. Iowa State has emerged as one of the most popular picks to challenge Michigan thanks to its elite defense and ability to force turnovers. Virginia and Tennessee also loom as dangerous teams capable of making a deep run if their strengths align during tournament play. Lower seeds could also make things interesting early. Teams like Santa Clara, Akron and Miami (Ohio) have the profiles of potential bracket busters, particularly if their shooting gets hot. With high-level contenders and upset possibilities throughout the bracket, the Midwest Region could deliver some of the most entertaining games of March Madness. When Is the NCAA Tournament Midwest Region 2026? Key Dates and Schedule For fans wondering when is March Madness Final Four action decided in the Midwest bracket, the road begins with the opening rounds of the NCAA Tournament. Gaming Insider Sign In Tom Wilkinson March 17th 2026 - 6 Min Read Midwest Region 2026: Odds, Predictions & Schedule Basketball • NCAAB The Midwest Region of the 2026 NCAA Tournament features a compelling mix of national title contenders, dangerous mid-seeds and potential Cinderella stories that could shake up the road to the NCAA Men's Final Four . Michigan enters the bracket as the No. 1 seed and one of the most complete teams in college basketball, powered by a dominant frontcourt and a balanced roster capable of controlling games inside and out. But this region is far from a one-team show. Iowa State has emerged as one of the most popular picks to challenge Michigan thanks to its elite defense and ability to force turnovers. Virginia and Tennessee also loom as dangerous teams capable of making a deep run if their strengths align during tournament play. Lower seeds could also make things interesting early. Teams like Santa Clara, Akron and Miami (Ohio) have the profiles of potential bracket busters, particularly if their shooting gets hot. With high-level contenders and upset possibilities throughout the bracket, the Midwest Region could deliver some of the most entertaining games of March Madness. modelTrace.content:
The KenPom page is truncated. Let me try a different approach - I have enough data from my earlier lookups and the Sports Reference page. Let me compile what I know:

Michigan:
- KenPom Rank: 2
- Torvik Rank: 2
- Record: 31-2
- AdjO: 126.7 (8th)
- AdjD: 88.4 (2nd)
- Adj EM: 38.38

Iowa State (from Sports Reference):
- Record: 27-7 (12-6, 3rd in Big 12)
- AP Poll: 7th (Mar 9)
- PS/G: 81.8 (53rd)
- PA/G: 65.1 (12th)
- SRS: 27.15 (5th)
- ORtg: 120.0 (21st)
- DRtg: 95.6 (6th)

So Michigan is clearly the superior team:
- #2 overall vs roughly #5-7 for Iowa State
- 31-2 record vs 27-7
- Better offense (8th vs 21st)
- Similar elite defense (2nd vs 6th)
- Much better efficiency margin (38.38 vs roughly 24-27 based on SRS)

From the prior picks in the bracket:
- Michigan was a 96% confidence pick over Howard in R64
- Michigan was an 84% confidence pick over Georgia in R32
- Michigan was a 73% confidence pick over Alabama in Sweet 16
- Iowa State was an 89% confidence pick over Tennessee State in R64
- Iowa State was a 72% confidence pick over Kentucky in R32
- Iowa State was a 56% confidence pick over Virginia in Sweet 16

This is an Elite 8 matchup between the #1 seed (Michigan) and #2 seed (Iowa State) in the Midwest. Michigan is the clear favorite based on:
1. Superior rankings (#2 vs ~#5-7)
2. Better record (31-2 vs 27-7)
3. Better offense (8th vs 21st)
4. Similar elite defense
5. Historical 1-seed advantage in Elite 8

However, Iowa State is no slouch - they have an elite defense (6th) and are a well-coached team under T.J. Otzelberger. This could be closer than a typical 1 vs 2 seed matchup.

I should make my pick now. Michigan is the clear favorite, but I'll set confidence at a moderate-high level (around 65-70%) since Iowa State is a quality opponent with elite defense that could keep this competitive.

Let me finalize my pick.
